name: Seared Tuna Steak
tagline: Sesame-crusted tuna seared rare, sliced thin, no shellfish anywhere
highlight: Hot pan, 90 seconds per side — rare ruby center
healthFacts: Ahi tuna is high in lean protein and selenium.
Black sesame seeds carry calcium and lignans — antioxidant compounds.

steps.title: Mix sesame seeds
steps.detail: Combine black and white sesame seeds with a pinch of salt on a plate.
steps.tip: A 50/50 mix gives the prettiest crust.

media.alt: Black and white sesame seeds on plate
media.caption: mix sesame seeds

steps.title: Crust the tuna
steps.detail: Pat tuna dry. Season lightly with salt. Press all sides into sesame seeds.
steps.tip: Press hard — seeds need to stick or they fall off in the pan.

steps.title: Sear blazing hot
steps.detail: Heat neutral oil in a non-stick or cast iron pan until just smoking. Sear tuna 60–90 sec per side. Pull at the first sign of doneness on the edge.
steps.tip: Center should still be ruby-red. This is sear, not cook-through.

steps.title: Slice thin, dip
steps.detail: Rest 1 min. Slice 1/4-inch thick across the grain. Whisk soy, vinegar, sesame oil for dipping.
steps.tip: A sharp knife is mandatory — dull saws push the rare center out.
